heading: Effective date
label: 179w Effective date
text: The provisions of sections 179 to 179w of this title shall apply to any copy of any film, including those copies of films selected for inclusion in the National Film Registry under the National Film Preservation Act of 1988 and the National Film Preservation Act of 1992, except that any film so selected under either Act shall be deemed to have been selected for the National Film Registry under sections 179 to 179w of this title.ll
notes: References in TextThe National Film Preservation Act of 1988, referred to in text, is , §§ 1–13, , , which was classified to sections 178 to 178 of this title and was repealed by , , .Pub. Amendments2008— struck out the first sentence which read as follows: “The provisions of sections 179 to 179w of this title shall be effective for 13 years beginning on .”Pub. L. 110–336lOctober 11, 19962005— substituted “13 years” for “7 years”.Pub. L. 109–9

Effective Date of 2008 AmendmentAmendment by effective as if included in the enactment of the National Film Preservation Act of 1996, , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 110–336Pub. L. 104–285, title Isection 3(a)(1)(C) of Pub. L. 110–336section 179v of this title

Effective Date Extension, , , provided that title I of , which enacted sections 179 to 179w of this title and repealed sections 179 to 179k of this title and provisions set out as a note under , was to be effective through fiscal year 2005, notwithstanding former provision of this section which provided that title I was effective for only 7 years beginning on .Pub.
